citation_title: Differential impact of FLASH and conventional radiotherapy on a pivotal metabolic organ: White Adipose Tissue
institution: Centro Pisano FLASH Radiotherapy (CPFR@CISUP), University of Pisa
notes_free_text: Scabia et al. 2026. bioRxiv preprint, posted April 1, 2026. is_preprint=1. C57BL/6J male, 6-7 weeks. Healthy normal tissue model — NO tumor. Target: inguinal subcutaneous white adipose tissue (iscWAT), proximal left hindlimb. ElectronFlash LINAC, CPFR@CISUP Pisa; 10mm circular collimator (~7mm effective ROI). Energy: 7-9 MeV (exact in vivo value not specified). FLASH: 940 Gy/s, 7 pulses × 4.75 Gy/pulse = 33.25 Gy (nominal 33.3 Gy), PRF=170 Hz, PW=4µs; irr-time ~41.2 ms (N/PRF). CONV: parameters not explicitly reported for this paper; same ElectronFlash LINAC platform as Furini 2026 (0.18 Gy/s, 0.19 Gy/pulse inferred). n=3-5 mice/group (stored as 4). Follow-up: 70 days. NOTE: Same machine/dose/pulse parameters as Furini 2026 Naive Arm 3 (but tissue = iscWAT, not hindlimb skin/muscle). IN VITRO ARMS EXCLUDED per user instruction. OUTCOMES (all WAT-specific): Fat mass: FLASH less reduction vs CONV. Adipocyte size: larger preserved with FLASH. PLIN1-negative adipocytes: fewer with FLASH (marker of lipid droplet loss). Degenerative vacuoles: fewer with FLASH. Macrophage infiltration: reduced with FLASH. Fibrosis: less with FLASH. Transcriptomics: FLASH 3 DEGs vs CONV 172 DEGs (98.3% reduction in transcriptomic disruption). Overall: CONV caused marked inflammatory and degenerative WAT changes; FLASH substantially preserved WAT structure, function, and gene expression. UNIQUE VALUE: One of very few WAT/metabolic-organ FLASH HTE papers in dataset. Strong mechanistic biology; clean FLASH vs CONV single-dose comparison.
data_notes: 33.3 Gy single-fraction localized left hind limb iscWAT, 7-9 MeV electron, ElectronFlash LINAC, Centro Pisano FLASH Radiotherapy (CPFR). FLASH ~240 Gy/s, ~1 Gy/pulse, ~180-225 Hz PRF, ~4 us pulse width, sub-second delivery. Male C57BL/6J, 6-7 weeks. 70-day follow-up. FLASH preserved: adipogenic potential, tissue architecture, mitochondrial structure, metabolic tissue integrity. Reduced senescence markers and inflammatory signaling. CONV caused: adipocyte degeneration, increased senescence, structural damage, CD68+ macrophage infiltration. Differences persisted to 70 days. In-vivo only arm — in-vitro SGBS adipocyte arms (4/8/16 Gy) NOT included here. Unique endpoint class in dataset: metabolic tissue (WAT) preservation.

citation_title: Early microglial activation in the TME enables FLASH-RT to eradicate medulloblastoma while promoting neuron-astrocyte crosstalk to minimize toxicity in the hippocampus
institution: University_of_Geneva_CHUV_Lausanne_Mexico_UCI_collaborators
notes_free_text: bioRxiv preprint March 18, 2026. is_preprint=1. Swiss nude (NU(Ico)-Foxn1nu) female, ~5 weeks. UW228 human medulloblastoma orthotopic cerebellum (stereotactic injection). Tumor confirmed by micro-CT prior to treatment. Oriatron eRT6, 6 MeV electron FLASH. 17mm half-circle graphite collimator, whole-brain, eye shielding. 3 × 10 Gy, 48h (2-day) inter-fraction intervals; total 30 Gy. FLASH: 5.5×10^6 Gy/s, single pulse per fraction; DPP=10 Gy; per-fx irr-time ~1.82 µs; total beam-on ~5.45 µs. IDR = FADR (single pulse per fraction). CONV: 0.1 Gy/s. PRF/PW not reported. n~16 FLASH tumor group; total study n=57 across cohorts. TUMOR: 100% eradication, durable long-term control, FLASH = CONV. COGNITION (NOR): FLASH 38% vs CONV 7% "very good discriminators" (+31pp; ~4.4× relative). NEUROINFLAM at 6 months: FLASH low microglial activation, reduced Iba1/CD68; CONV elevated inflammatory markers and persistent glial activation. MECHANISTIC (DSP + RNAseq): TME (cerebellum): FLASH induced phagocytic/proteolytic/tissue-clearance microglial programs; key genes Megf10, Mmp16, Adam19. Hippocampus: FLASH induced neuron-astrocyte crosstalk, synaptic plasticity, metabolic preservation, reduced chronic neuroinflam. Regions analyzed: DG, CA1, CA3. Extremely high FADR (5.5×10^6 Gy/s) — extreme UHDR regime vs typical ~100 Gy/s FLASH. High-value mechanistic paper for causal/ML modeling: spatial transcriptomics, microglial response, neurocognitive endpoints, tumor eradication.
data_notes: 3x10 Gy every 48h (30 Gy total), 6 MeV electron, Oriatron eRT6, whole brain. FLASH 5.5e6 Gy/s (single pulse per fraction) vs CONV 0.1 Gy/s. Swiss nude female mice, 5 weeks at RT. UW228 orthotopic medulloblastoma, stereotactic implant (AP-6, ML+1, DV+2 mm, 30°), 1e6 cells, 2 weeks to RT. 17 mm semicircular graphite collimator, eyes shielded. Dual outcome: FLASH eradicates medulloblastoma AND preserves hippocampal function. FLASH: reduced neuroinflammation, preserved cognitive function, promoted neuron-astrocyte crosstalk. CONV: measurable cognitive/inflammatory effects. Early microglial activation in TME proposed as FLASH mechanism for tumor eradication. Only 1 FLASH arm — no dose or fractionation variations.

citation_title: Neuroprotection of Radiosensitive Juvenile Mice by Ultra-High Dose Rate FLASH Irradiation
institution: University of California Irvine; Lausanne University Hospital
notes_free_text: Alaghband 2020 F1. Juvenile C57BL/6J mice (3 weeks old, mixed sex), WBI 8 Gy single fraction. 6 MeV Oriatron eRT6. FLASH: 4.4e6 Gy/s, 1 pulse at 1.8µs, 8 Gy/pulse, PRF=100 Hz. CONV: 0.077 Gy/s, 1033 pulses at 1.0µs, 10 Hz, 0.00774 Gy/pulse, 103.2s total. n=16 behavioral, n=4-6 histology per group. Follow-up 2 and 4 months. FLASH preserved: OUL memory, NOR, social interaction, fear extinction, DCX+ neurons, BrdU+NeuN+ neurogenesis, microglial activation, plasma GH. Juvenile tumor-free WBI model — relevant to pediatric medulloblastoma toxicity context. No tumor arm. DMF not calculable.
data_notes: 8 Gy single-fraction WBI, 6 MeV electrons, Oriatron eRT6. FLASH ~4.4e6 Gy/s instantaneous (1 pulse, 1.8 us) vs CONV ~0.077 Gy/s. Juvenile C57BL/6J (3 weeks), n=16/group. Follow-up 2 and 4 months. FLASH preserved novel object location, recognition, social interaction, fear extinction, and growth hormone levels vs CONV-impaired. Key finding: juvenile mice (more radiosensitive) show FLASH neurocognitive sparing. Reduced neuroinflammation markers with FLASH. Endpoints: OUL, NOR, LDB, SIT, fear extinction, GH, neuroinflammation.

citation_title: Neuroprotective Effects of Ultra-High Dose Rate FLASH Bragg Peak Proton Irradiation
institution: Heidelberg University Hospital; DKFZ; Heidelberg Ion-Beam Therapy Center (HIT)
machine_name: HIT synchrotron active raster-scanning proton system
notes_free_text: Dokic 2022 F1. C57BL/6 female, healthy brain Bragg peak irradiation. 146.6 MeV/u proton, HIT synchrotron raster-scan. 9 spots, 6x6mm PMMA collimator. Bragg peak at ~3.9mm depth. LETD 4.5-10.2 keV/um proximal-to-distal. FLASH: 120 Gy/s, 1 spill, 0.08s. SDR comparator: 0.17 Gy/s mean, 10 spills ~58s, SDR inst spill DR=38.8 Gy/s. n=5/group/timepoint. FLASH vs SDR: reduced gH2AX+ fraction, reduced gH2AX intensity, better CD31+ microvascular preservation, less CD68+ inflammatory cells, reduced HMGB1 nuclear loss. All endpoints favor FLASH at 1h and 7d. Healthy-tissue-only study; first proton Bragg peak FLASH neuroprotection paper.
data_notes: ~9.6 Gy single-fraction right-hemisphere proton Bragg peak, HIT Heidelberg. FLASH 120 Gy/s (~0.08 s delivery) vs CONV 0.17 Gy/s (~58 s). C57BL/6. Follow-up 1h and 7 days. FLASH significantly reduced: CD68+ microglial activation, HMGB1 release, and persistent gamma-H2AX DNA damage foci vs CONV at 7 days. Neuroinflammation-specific endpoint — distinct from cognition (Simmons_2019), gliosis (MontayGruel_2021), and behavioral endpoints (Alaghband_2020). peak_toxicity_grade=not_applicable: inflammatory marker endpoint, not graded toxicity. Single radiation condition — 1 arm only.

citation_title: Pencil beam scanning proton FLASH maintains tumor control while normal tissue damage is reduced in a mouse model
institution: Danish Centre for Particle Therapy, Aarhus University Hospital; Department of Experimental Clinical Oncology, Aarhus University Hospital; DTU Health Tech, Roskilde; Department of Oncology, Aarhus University Hospital, Denmark
notes_free_text: Sorensen 2022. CDF1 female, C3H mammary carcinoma hind limb foot. Single-fraction PBS proton dose-response (40–60 Gy range). No anesthesia during irradiation. ProBeam Varian. FLASH: 250 MeV, 71–89 Gy/s (mean 83), DRPBS95%=171–214 Gy/s, irr=0.46–0.84 s, n=70/75/35. CONV: 244 MeV, 0.33–0.63 Gy/s (mean 0.38), irr=95–173 s, n=72/80/36. FLASH 215 nA, no repainting; CONV 1.1 nA, 327–488 repaintings. Tumor control: TCD50_FLASH=51.3 Gy vs TCD50_CONV=49.1 Gy → DMF=1.04, p=0.50 (NS). Acute skin: MDD50_FLASH=52.3 Gy vs CONV MDD50 <40.1 Gy → DMF≥1.30 (acute). Fibrosis: FD50_FLASH=55.6 Gy vs FD50_CONV=48.6 Gy → DMF=1.14, p=0.0001. Fibrosis DMF (1.14) significantly less than published acute-skin DMF (1.50, p<0.05). Entrance plateau region (low LET); no SOBP effects. Beam geometry: entrance depth 13.5 cm, brass body shield, no collimator. Fibrosis analysis: only tumor-control mice included; limited n per dose group (limitation).
data_notes: PBS proton FLASH tumor control arm. TCD50_FLASH=51.3 Gy vs TCD50_CONV=49.1 Gy. FLASH approximately equivalent to CONV for tumor control. Dose range: FLASH 40.4-60.2 Gy, CONV 40.1-59.7 Gy. Confirms therapeutic equivalence of PBS proton FLASH.

citation_title: Preliminary mechanistic study of mitochondrial function in intestinal protection mediated by high-energy X-ray FLASH radiotherapy
institution: Mianyang Central Hospital; University of Electronic Science and Technology of China; Zhongjiu Flash Medical Technology Co. Ltd.
notes_free_text: Hao 2026. C57BL/6 female, 6-8 weeks, 12 Gy WAbdomen single fraction. CHEX-FLASH S-band linac; 10 MeV e- → rotating W target → MV X-ray. FLASH 200 Gy/s; CONV 0.185 Gy/s. Measured dose: 12.01±0.12 Gy. 4×4 cm field; 1 cm water-eq bolus dorsal; prone on 0.5 cm PMMA. PRF/PW/DPP not reported; irr_time not reported (estimated ~0.06s from DR only). n=5 mechanistic (72h), n=10 survival (15d). SURVIVAL: FLASH 80% (8/10) vs CONV 30% (3/10); +50pp absolute (+166.7% relative; RR_death=0.286). Body weight: milder decline and better recovery with FLASH. HISTOLOGY: villus height FLASH 335.99±45.81µm ≈ control 343.62±36.20µm > CONV. Crypt density intermediate FLASH vs lower CONV. Injury score lower FLASH. Ki-67 higher FLASH. γH2AX: no difference between irradiated groups. Apoptosis differences not clearly significant. MITOCHONDRIA (TEM): much less structural damage FLASH vs CONV. MMP better preserved FLASH; lower ROS FLASH; lower Parkin/PINK1/LC3 (less mitophagy). METABOLOMICS: 8 differential metabolites FLASH vs CONV (6 up, 2 down); enriched in glycine/serine/threonine and taurine/hypotaurine metabolism (mitochondrial antioxidant). Malonic acid (mitochondrial damage marker) lower in FLASH. NRF2 higher in FLASH; total SOD lower in FLASH. CHEX-FLASH = compact HEX FLASH platform; same particle class as Li 2025.
data_notes: 12 Gy WAbdomen, 10 MV HEX photon, CHEX-FLASH (Zhongjiu), S-band compact RF linac. FLASH 200 Gy/s vs CONV 0.185 Gy/s. Female C57BL/6, 6-8 weeks. Isoflurane. 4x4 cm field, prone, 1 cm bolus, ~50 cm SSD. Gafchromic EBT-XD film dosimetry. Survival at day 15: FLASH 80% vs CONV 30% (+50 pp). Histology: FLASH reduced villous atrophy, epithelial damage, injury score vs CONV. Villus height FLASH 335.99 um vs CONV lower (p=0.0037). Ki-67 FLASH >> CONV. Mechanistic: FLASH preserved mitochondrial morphology and membrane potential, reduced ROS, less mitophagy (Parkin/PINK1/LC3), NRF2 higher in FLASH. Malonic acid significantly lower in FLASH vs CONV (metabolomics). Antioxidant mitochondrial metabolic pathways enriched in FLASH. gamma-H2AX: no significant FLASH vs CONV difference at 72h.

FLASH vs CONV at 21 Gy: preserved villus-crypt architecture; reduced epithelial apoptosis; preserved endothelial cells; lowered barrier injury markers; preserved goblet/mucus/tight-junction markers; preserved CBC+/Olfm4+/Lgr5+ ISC populations; increased revSC abundance; enhanced Wnt/stemness programs; reduced oxidative stress and DNA damage; delayed ISC cell-cycle progression (interpreted as protective). machine_name: SNAKE beamline, 14 MV tandem accelerator, Maier-Leibnitz-Laboratorium Garching
notes_free_text: Rudigkeit 2024 F4. BALB/c female, right ear skin, 33 Gy Flash930. 20.5 MeV protons, LET=2.6 keV/µm. SNAKE beamline. Flash930: mean DR=930 Gy/s, continuous beam. irr_time=0.035 s. n=9. Conv=0.06 Gy/s. Peak ear swelling: Conv=155±38 µm, Flash930 reduced by 40±13% → ~93 µm est. Peak inflammation score: Conv=2.4±0.6, Flash930 reduced by 50±17% → ~1.2 est. p=0.16 (swelling), p=0.13 (inflammation) — NOT statistically significant. Blood cytokines: no difference. Flash930 shows less sparing than Flash9 at 33 Gy (40% vs 57% swelling reduction), suggesting effect may plateau between 9.3 and 930 Gy/s. Authors note irradiated blood volume Flash930=0.20±0.07 µl vs Conv=27±7 µl.
data_notes: 33 Gy, Flash930 (930 Gy/s). Mild FLASH skin sparing (trend, not significant). Ear swelling: -40% vs CONV. Inflammation score: -50% vs CONV. Less protective than Flash9 at 33 Gy — counterintuitive dose-rate relationship.

citation_title: Radiation damage to normal mammalian tissue in vivo with laser-driven protons at ultra-high instantaneous dose rate
institution: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (BELLA PW facility)
energy: 5-9 MeV spectrum at sample; peak 8 MeV; avg LET 6.9±2.0 keV/um
machine_name: BELLA PW laser-driven proton beamline (TNSA), Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
notes_free_text: LBNL BELLA PW laser-driven proton UHIDR study. Year not specified. BALB/cJ female mice, left ear pinna normal skin irradiation. LASER-DRIVEN PROTON (TNSA): 5-9 MeV spectrum, peak 8 MeV; LET=6.9±2.0 keV/um. 8mm diameter beam. Dose per bunch ~2.0±0.4 Gy; bunch length 11 ns; bunch separation ~20 s; IDR=1.3×10^8 Gy/s. IMPORTANT: MEAN dose rate = ~0.1 Gy/s (far below FLASH threshold of 40 Gy/s). This is NOT a canonical FLASH arm. Extreme IDR but sub-FLASH mean DR. Comparator is X-ray (not matched proton CONV) — LET and dose differ. Group A: 36.0 Gy, 1 fraction, 18 bunches, session ~360s, n~7. Peak ear thickness: 286±28 µm at day 24. Lowest swelling among 4 proton groups. Vs 40.8 Gy X-ray comparator (372 µm peak): -23.1% swelling; vs 35.1 Gy X-ray: moderately lower (p~0.05 days 18-46), exact values NR. RNAseq d28: cytokine, antigen presentation, keratinocyte differentiation upregulated. RNAseq d84: immune enrichment largely absent; persistent keratinocyte/proliferative activity. effect_size stored cautiously; non-iso-dose X-ray comparator.
data_notes: 36 Gy, 1fx, Group A. BELLA PW laser-driven proton, 8 MeV, ~1.3e8 Gy/s inst. Low swelling, full recovery. Less toxicity than X-ray reference cohorts. Mild FLASH-like benefit. No direct CONV comparator — inferred from X-ray reference.

citation_title: Radiation damage to normal mammalian tissue in vivo with laser-driven protons at ultra-high instantaneous dose rate
institution: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (BELLA PW facility)
energy: 5-9 MeV spectrum at sample; peak 8 MeV; avg LET 6.9±2.0 keV/um
machine_name: BELLA PW laser-driven proton beamline (TNSA), Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
notes_free_text: LBNL BELLA PW laser-driven proton UHIDR. Group B: 42.1 Gy, 2 fractions (~21.05 Gy/fx), ~21 bunches total, n~7. Mean DR ~0.1 Gy/s (NOT FLASH). IDR=1.3×10^8 Gy/s during each 11ns bunch. Peak ear thickness: 298±35 µm at day 28. Similar skin response to Group A (286±28 µm). +4.2% vs Group A peak swelling — no significant fractionation benefit. Erythema/desquamation pattern similar to Group A. Authors conclude: fractionation did not show significant protective effect in this UHIDR proton regime. Inter-fraction interval not reported. No X-ray matched comparator for this arm.
data_notes: 42.1 Gy, 2fx, Group B. BELLA PW laser-driven proton. Fractionation did not reduce toxicity. No clear FLASH advantage over Group A. Aligns with fractionation collapse seen in electron and proton-repainting FLASH studies. HTE=UNCLEAR — inferred from X-ray comparison only.

citation_title: Radiation damage to normal mammalian tissue in vivo with laser-driven protons at ultra-high instantaneous dose rate
institution: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (BELLA PW facility)
energy: 5-9 MeV spectrum at sample; peak 8 MeV; avg LET 6.9±2.0 keV/um
machine_name: BELLA PW laser-driven proton beamline (TNSA), Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
notes_free_text: LBNL BELLA PW laser-driven proton UHIDR. Group C: 39.6 Gy, 4 fractions (~9.9 Gy/fx), ~20 bunches total, n~7. Mean DR ~0.1 Gy/s (NOT FLASH). IDR=1.3×10^8 Gy/s during each 11ns bunch. Peak ear thickness: 316±28 µm at day 21. +10.5% vs Group A (286 µm); +6.0% vs Group B (298 µm). Still within authors' range of no significant fractionation advantage among A-C. Confirms no clear normal-tissue benefit from splitting dose across 4 fractions in this UHIDR laser-proton regime. Inter-fraction interval not reported.
data_notes: 39.6 Gy, 4fx, Group C. BELLA PW laser-driven proton. More fractions did not protect tissue — consistent with temporal structure sensitivity. HTE=UNCLEAR — no improvement from 4fx over 2fx. Confirms FLASH sparing sensitive to time structure in laser-driven regime.

citation_title: Radiation damage to normal mammalian tissue in vivo with laser-driven protons at ultra-high instantaneous dose rate
institution: Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory (BELLA PW facility)
energy: 5-9 MeV spectrum at sample; peak 8 MeV; avg LET 6.9±2.0 keV/um
machine_name: BELLA PW laser-driven proton beamline (TNSA), Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
notes_free_text: LBNL BELLA PW laser-driven proton UHIDR. Group D: 50.6 Gy, single fraction, ~25 bunches, n~7. Mean DR ~0.1 Gy/s (NOT FLASH). IDR=1.3×10^8 Gy/s during each 11ns bunch. Peak ear thickness: 468±260 µm at day 27. Large SD reflects heterogeneity. +63.6% vs Group A (286 µm); +48.1% vs Group C (316 µm). 2 animals reached combined score 3.0 (erythema+desquamation); others 0.5-1.0. Most injurious proton arm — clear dose-threshold effect above Groups A-C. RNAseq d84: downregulated genes strongly enriched for immune/leukocyte/chemotaxis; few enriched upregulated terms; interpreted as broad late-phase attenuation/functional exhaustion at high dose. Contrasts with Group A (d84: epidermal/proliferative activity). HTE=NO: no sparing signal; suprathreshold damage.
data_notes: 50.6 Gy, 1fx, Group D. BELLA PW laser-driven proton. Severe reactions — some animals reached score 3.0, others 0.5-1.0. High-dose toxicity dominated; no sparing observed at this dose. Confirms dose threshold above which laser-driven FLASH cannot spare skin.

citation_title: Reduced cognitive deficits after FLASH irradiation of whole mouse brain are associated with less hippocampal dendritic spine loss and neuroinflammation
institution: Stanford University School of Medicine; Jackson Laboratory Sacramento; Vanderbilt University; Stanford Cancer Institute; Indiana University School of Medicine
energy: 16 MeV and 20 MeV (not separated into biological sub-arms)
notes_free_text: Simmons 2019. C57BL/6J male 3mo, whole brain lateral irradiation, 30 Gy single fx. Varian Clinac 21EX (custom FLASH). 16+20 MeV electrons (not separated into sub-arms). FLASH: 200-300 Gy/s (MDR), IDR=8.75e5 Gy/s, PRF=108-180 Hz, PW=2 µs, ~18 pulses, DPP=1.75 Gy. Conv: 0.13 Gy/s, 240 s delivery. KEY DESIGN: DPP, pulse count, beam geometry IDENTICAL between FLASH and CONV; only PRF/MDR/total delivery time differed. FLASH measured dose: 30.4±0.37 Gy; Conv: 30.8±0.09 Gy. Outcomes (10 wk follow-up): FLASH preserved novel object location + recognition (Conv significantly impaired vs control, FLASH not). Dendritic spine: Conv reduced apical spine density -18±4.9% vs control (p sig); FLASH not significantly reduced. CD68: control=132.2±58.4, Conv=379±45.5, FLASH=255.4±47.9 cells/mouse; FLASH vs Conv = -32.6% (NS by p-value but biologically intermediate). Cytokines: Conv elevated IL-6, IL-1b, TNFa vs control; FLASH showed fewer/smaller cytokine increases. n: behavioral 26FLASH/25CONV/20ctrl; spines 8/8/5; CD68 5/6/6; cytokines 10/7/10.
data_notes: 30 Gy single-fraction WBI, FLASH vs CONV vs control. 10-week follow-up. FLASH-treated mice showed preserved novel object recognition and fear conditioning vs CONV-impaired. Less hippocampal dendritic spine loss and reduced neuroinflammation with FLASH. Mechanistically links FLASH sparing to structural synaptic preservation and microglial activation reduction.

institution: University of British Columbia; University of Victoria; TRIUMF, Canada
machine_name: FIRST 10 MV UHDR photon source at TRIUMF
notes_free_text: Ford 2025 F1. C57BL/6 male, healthy thorax, 15 Gy prescribed. FIRST 10 MV photon FLASH (TRIUMF). 1×1 cm² field, lateral prone. FLASH DR=89.79±7.67 Gy/s; CONV DR=0.036±0.003 Gy/s. FLASH delivered dose=14.57±0.80 Gy; CONV delivered=11.74±0.76 Gy (dose mismatch noted). n=6, 6/6 survived to 24 wk. Micro-CT fibrosis at endpoint: FLASH 2/6=33% vs CONV 1/6=17%. Histology fibrosis: FLASH 3/6=50% vs CONV 1/6=17%. Pneumonitis: 1 mouse each at 12 wk (both groups). No convincing FLASH sparing at 15 Gy; fibrosis numerically higher in FLASH group. FRC and VT preserved in both 15 Gy groups. Note: 10 MV photon FLASH from TRIUMF FIRST platform — rare particle/energy in dataset. Pulse parameters not reported.
data_notes: 15 Gy thoracic FLASH (~90 Gy/s) vs CONV (~0.04 Gy/s). No FLASH sparing. Fibrosis: FLASH 3/6 mice vs CONV 1/6 mice (-33.3 pp, trend against FLASH). Low-level sporadic fibrosis in both groups. No significant difference in quantitative lung measurements. HTE=NO.

citation_title: Respiratory-gated micro-computed tomography imaging to measure radiation-induced lung injuries in mice following ultra-high dose-rate and conventional dose-rate radiation therapy
institution: University of British Columbia; University of Victoria; TRIUMF, Canada
machine_name: FIRST 10 MV UHDR photon source at TRIUMF
notes_free_text: Ford 2025 F2. C57BL/6 male, healthy thorax, 30 Gy prescribed. FIRST 10 MV photon FLASH (TRIUMF). 1×1 cm² field, lateral prone. FLASH DR=97.58±14.12 Gy/s; CONV DR=0.06±0.005 Gy/s. FLASH delivered dose=29.65±1.57 Gy. n=6; 5/6 FLASH survived to 18 wk endpoint; 1 euthanized at 16 wk with pneumonitis. CONV: 4/6 survived to endpoint. Survival-based effect_size=25%=(83.3%-66.7%)/66.7%. Micro-CT fibrosis at endpoint survivors: FLASH 4/5=80% vs CONV 1/4=25% (biased: sicker CONV animals lost before endpoint). Histology fibrosis including early losses: FLASH ~5/6, CONV ~5/6 but CONV denser/more extensive. FRC: 30 Gy FLASH reduced ~50% vs control at endpoint; VT reduced ~47% vs control. FLASH vs CONV: no statistically significant direct difference for lung measurements, but conventional showed larger volume reduction and denser fibrosis qualitatively. Effect direction: weak qualitative sparing only. Confounds: GI irradiation caused gastroenteritis in both 30 Gy groups; endpoint survivor bias affects fibrosis incidence counts. Pulse params not reported.
data_notes: 30 Gy thoracic FLASH (~98 Gy/s) vs CONV (~0.06 Gy/s). Mild/partial FLASH lung sparing. CONV: more extensive dense fibrosis (4/4 endpoint mice + 1 early euthanized with extensive damage). FLASH: less extensive fibrosis (4/5 endpoint mice + 1 early euthanized with regional damage). No significant difference in quantitative CT measurements. Histological sparing only — coded as mild. No DMF calculable.

institution: PLA General Hospital / PLA Medical School; Institute of Applied Electronics CAEP; National Key Laboratory on Advanced Laser and High Power Microwave; Zhongjiu Flash Medical Technology Co. Ltd, China

notes_free_text: He 2026 F1. C57BL/6J male, whole-brain 10 Gy. FLASH: 213 Gy/s, 10 MV x-ray, Zhongjiu RF linac, 1.2×2 cm field, SSD=43 cm, irr_time=47 ms. CONV: Elekta Precise 6 MV, 0.033 Gy/s (2 Gy/min), 1.2×30 cm field, SSD=100 cm, irr_time=300 s. n=16/group initial; no deaths in either 10 Gy group. FLASH 10 Gy showed better delayed cognitive outcomes vs CONV 10 Gy: elevated plus maze, Y-maze, novel object recognition preserved. Hippocampal NeuN neurons preserved; GFAP astrocytes less activated. Quantitative effect sizes not calculable from text (no group means reported). Pulse structure: macropulse RF linac; exact per-pulse parameters not extractable.
data_notes: 10 Gy WBI, 10 MV FLASH (213 Gy/s, 47 ms) vs 6 MV CONV (0.033 Gy/s, 300 s). CONV: measurable anxiety-like/cognitive impairment, hippocampal astrocyte activation, neuronal loss. FLASH: near-sham performance. Moderate cognitive sparing. Effect size calculable from behavioral figures. No DMF.

citation_title: The Impacts of FLASH Radiation Therapy and Conventional Radiation Therapy on the Cognitive Abilities of Mice
institution: PLA General Hospital / PLA Medical School; Institute of Applied Electronics CAEP; National Key Laboratory on Advanced Laser and High Power Microwave; Zhongjiu Flash Medical Technology Co. Ltd, China
machine_name: Miniaturized x-ray FLASH RF linac (Zhongjiu Flash Medical Technology)
notes_free_text: He 2026 F2. C57BL/6J male, whole-brain 20 Gy. FLASH: 213 Gy/s, 10 MV x-ray, Zhongjiu RF linac, irr_time=91 ms. CONV: Elekta Precise 6 MV, 0.033 Gy/s, irr_time=600 s. n initial=16/group. 30-day survival: FLASH=87.5% (14/16), CONV=25% (4/16). Relative survival improvement = 250% = (87.5-25)/25×100. Absolute survival difference = 62.5 pp. Behavioral analysis n: FLASH n=12, CONV n=4 (small CONV n is key limitation). CONV 20 Gy: hair loss, ulceration, erythema, swelling, crusting by d30; follicular damage by d60. FLASH 20 Gy: no lesions at d30, mild hair loss only by d60. Cognition: FLASH markedly better on all 4 behavioral tasks vs CONV. Oxidative stress: CONV higher MDA; FLASH higher SOD, T-AOC, GSH-PX. Authors caution: CONV 20 Gy behavioral n=4 limits robustness of cognitive comparisons.
data_notes: 20 Gy WBI, 10 MV FLASH (213 Gy/s, 91 ms) vs 6 MV CONV (0.033 Gy/s, 600 s). 30-day survival: FLASH 87.5% vs CONV 25% (+62.5 pp, +250% relative). FLASH also reduced radiodermatitis, preserved cognition, reduced hippocampal astrocyte activation, preserved neurons, reduced oxidative stress. Strong multi-endpoint benefit. No DMF.

PRF/PW/DPP/irr_time not reported; macropulse duration varied to adjust dose. n=12 behavior, n=6 metabolomics/microbiome/timepoint, n=5 GFAP/Iba1 histology, n=3 CD68 CA1, n=6 CBF. Follow-up: day 1/3/7/21; behavior day 16-20; sacrifice day 21. BEHAVIOR: FLASH closer to sham than CONV in NOR, Y-maze, OFT, EPM at day 21. NEUROINFLAMMATION: lower GFAP and Iba1 in FLASH vs CONV across hippocampus and cortex; less CD68+ microglial accumulation in CA1. CBF: higher cerebral blood flow in FLASH vs CONV at day 21. METABOLOMICS: FLASH shows late-stage rebound/restoration of amino acid, carbohydrate, neurotransmission pathways vs persistent late suppression in CONV. MICROBIOTA: FLASH milder/more transient dysbiosis; faster recovery toward sham; earlier reduction of Proteobacteria/Enterobacteriaceae disruption; later enrichment of commensal/beneficial taxa. Weak for quantitative ML (no numeric endpoint means; total dose missing); strong for qualitative HTE classification and mechanistic feature encoding.
data_notes: 20 Gy WBI, 10 MV HEX photons. FLASH 200 Gy/s vs CONV 0.066 Gy/s. Single radiation condition, multiple timepoint follow-up. FLASH produced: less hippocampal microglial activation, less astrocyte activation, smaller reduction in cerebral blood flow, reduced cognitive deficits vs CONV. FLASH=mild but detectable changes (severity=1); CONV=clear measurable injury (severity=3). Also characterizes brain metabolite and gut microbiota alterations post-irradiation. Multi-endpoint mechanistic study. peak_toxicity_grade=not_applicable: functional endpoints. total_dose_Gy corrected from blank to 20 Gy.

FLASH sparing has an upper dose limit for normal lung in this model; dose ceiling approximately 20-22 Gy for lung.
data_notes: 23-30 Gy FLASH upper limit arm. Loss-of-protection boundary. At ~30 Gy, FLASH fibrosis equals CONV 17 Gy fibrosis. Defines ceiling of FLASH therapeutic window. DMF=1.76 curve-level. Mechanistically important: above this dose FLASH protection exhausted.

institution: Institut Curie; INSERM; Institut Gustave-Roussy; Université Paris-XI; Ecole Nationale Vétérinaire d'Alfort; French/Swiss collaborating institutions
notes_free_text: Favaudon 2014 STM. C57BL/6J female, lung, 17 Gy FLASH vs 7.5 Gy CONV for apoptosis assay. 4.5 MeV LINAC, FLASH 60 Gy/s; CONV 137Cs gamma. 1h and 24h endpoints. 7.5 Gy CONV: strong apoptosis in vascular and bronchial smooth muscle cells. 17 Gy FLASH: essentially no cleaved caspase-3 or TUNEL signal. 30 Gy FLASH needed to produce apoptosis similar to 7.5 Gy CONV. DMF_apoptosis = 30/7.5 = 4.0. 30 Gy FLASH also tested for this endpoint.
data_notes: 17 Gy FLASH vs CONV. Reduced endothelial apoptosis (caspase-3/TUNEL) with FLASH. Mechanistic arm. Endothelial protection proposed as upstream driver of fibrosis sparing. DMF not applicable here — endpoint is apoptosis frequency, not fibrosis dose-response.

citation_title: Ultrahigh dose-rate FLASH irradiation increases the differential response between normal and tumor tissue in mice
institution: Institut Curie; INSERM; Institut Gustave-Roussy; Université Paris-XI; Ecole Nationale Vétérinaire d'Alfort; French/Swiss collaborating institutions
notes_free_text: Favaudon 2014 STM. C57BL/6J female, lung, 30 Gy FLASH + TNF-α (systemic, 24h before RT). CONV comparison: 15 Gy CONV + TNF-α. 4.5 MeV LINAC, FLASH 60 Gy/s. TNF-α increased apoptosis and triggered pulmonary edema in both groups. TNF-α reversed acute apoptosis-sparing benefit of FLASH (dramatic edema). However, TNF-α did not induce lung fibrosis in FLASH-irradiated animals; fibrosis patches seen with 15 Gy CONV + TNF-α. Supports vascular/apoptotic contribution to FLASH effect but not as sole explanation.
data_notes: 30 Gy FLASH vs CONV. Reduced TNF-alpha expression in lung tissue with FLASH. Mechanistic arm linking reduced inflammation to downstream fibrosis protection. Supports oxygen depletion / radical scavenging model.

citation_title: Ultrahigh dose-rate FLASH irradiation increases the differential response between normal and tumor tissue in mice
institution: Institut Curie; INSERM; Institut Gustave-Roussy; Université Paris-XI; Ecole Nationale Vétérinaire d'Alfort; French/Swiss collaborating institutions
notes_free_text: Favaudon 2014 STM. C57BL/6J female, TC-1 Luc+ orthotopic lung carcinoma, 28 Gy FLASH. Tumor implanted 2d before irradiation. n=10. 4.5 MeV LINAC, 60 Gy/s. 28 Gy FLASH vs 15 Gy CONV both initially suppressed tumor, but most CONV mice progressed by 28-35 days. 80% of 28 Gy FLASH mice alive at 62 days; 60% tumor-free. CONV survivors: inflammatory/fibrotic lung remodeling. FLASH survivors: microscopically normal lungs, no inflammatory infiltration, no extracellular matrix deposition. KEY therapeutic gain arm: escalated FLASH dose achieves better tumor control PLUS substantially less thoracic normal-tissue damage. Compares 28 Gy FLASH vs 15 Gy CONV (note: unmatched doses).
data_notes: TC1 orthotopic lung tumor, 28 Gy FLASH. Hybrid arm: tumor control preserved while lung normal tissue spared relative to CONV. Near upper FLASH limit (30 Gy). Demonstrates therapeutic window widening: tumor controlled at dose that would cause severe CONV lung fibrosis. DMF=1.76 curve-level.

citation_title: Whole Abdominal Pencil Beam Scanned Proton FLASH Increases Acute Lethality
institution: Albert Einstein College of Medicine; New York Proton Center; Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center
notes_free_text: WAbdPBS 2024. C57BL/6J male+female, whole abdomen 14 Gy single fraction PBS proton. Varian ProBeam, 250 MeV, 2.5×4 cm field, 6×9 spots, 5mm spacing, 3.5mm sigma. FLASH FADR=82.01±3.09 Gy/s; CONV FADR=0.59±0.03 Gy/s (ratio ≈139-fold). Treatment time: FLASH 0.171±0.0052s vs CONV 23.87±1.2s (ratio ≈140-fold). Spot peak IDR=873.4 Gy/s at 215 nA; mean LADR=196-240 Gy/s (>100 Gy/s in ≥90% dose region). n=25 CONV, n=23 FLASH. Survival to 14 days. KEY RESULT: FLASH significantly worsened survival vs CONV. Log-rank P=0.0003; dose-adjusted Cox P=0.0006. KM plot shows markedly earlier/more frequent death in FLASH arm. NEGATIVE/CAUTIONARY: PBS proton FLASH increases acute lethality in this whole-abdomen model.
data_notes: 14 Gy PBS proton, whole-abdomen. FLASH significantly reduced 14-day survival vs CONV. Increased acute lethality — one of the clearest harmful FLASH results in GI literature. FADR ~79-107 Gy/s insufficient for GI sparing with PBS delivery. HTE=NO.

institution: MD_Anderson
notes_free_text: Saenz et al. 2025. bioRxiv preprint, posted April 11, 2025. MD Anderson. Mobetron 9 MeV electron FLASH, 3x9Gy=27Gy total, 270 Gy/s, 3 Gy/pulse, PRF=90 Hz (calculated). mEER HPV+ oropharyngeal SCC, subcutaneous flank, C57BL/6J female mice. n=7-10/group. Key immune fold-changes FLASH vs CONV: DLN CD8+ 2.13x; DLN CD4+ 1.92x; naive CD8+ 2.04x; effector CD8+ 4.4x; effector CD4+ 3.67x; macrophages 3.03x; MHC-II+ macrophages 3.20x; monocytes 1.82x; dendritic cells 2.22x; tumor CD8+ density 3.44x (57.4 vs 16.7 cells/mm2); PD1+CD8+ 5.0x (31.0% vs 6.2%); PDL1+ macrophages 20.9x (11.3% vs 0.54%). CONV comparator 0.2 Gy/s. Anesthesia/field size not reported. is_preprint=1.
data_notes: Electron UHDR, 3x9 Gy daily fractions (27 Gy total), mEER HPV+ flank tumor, C57BL/6. FLASH >40 Gy/s vs CONV 0.01-0.5 Gy/s. IMPORTANT: hypofractionated UHDR — no single-fraction comparison. Immune analysis 5 days post-irradiation. CD8+ T cells in DLNs: FLASH 1.7e4 vs CONV 0.8e4. CD4+ T cells in DLNs: FLASH 2.3e4 vs CONV 1.2e4. Naive and activated lymphocytes higher in FLASH vs CONV. Tumor infiltrating lymphocytes increased with FLASH. PD1 expression and PDL1+ macrophages increased with FLASH. Stereotactic jig aligned tumor to minimize DLN overlap with field. FLASH preserves anti-tumor immune axis while delivering equivalent local dose.

institution: John Hopkins
data_notes: 21 Gy ocular X-ray FLASH. ERG: FLASH retinal function near baseline, CONV showed reduced amplitudes (moderate impairment). No DMF calculable — 2 fixed doses only, no iso-effect curve. 2-month follow-up. functional_delta=2.

citation_title: A Feasibility Study of Preclinical Ocular X-Ray FLASH Radiation Therapy
institution: John Hopkins
machine_name: Varex Imaging Rad44 rotating anode x-ray
data_notes: 26 Gy ocular X-ray FLASH. ERG + histology: FLASH retained electrical activity, structurally intact retina. CONV: severe functional loss, blindness, massive photoreceptor loss, retinal necrosis. Strong FLASH sparing. No DMF calculable. functional_delta=2.

citation_title: Abdominopelvic FLASH Irradiation Improves PD-1 Immune Checkpoint Inhibition in Preclinical Models of Ovarian Cancer
institution: Stanford
data_notes: UPK10 ovarian model, 14 Gy abdominopelvic FLASH, no co-tx. Tumor weight reduced similarly by aPD-1, CONV, FLASH, and combinations. No separate normal-tissue toxicity comparison for this model/arm.

citation_title: Abdominopelvic FLASH Irradiation Improves PD-1 Immune Checkpoint Inhibition in Preclinical Models of Ovarian Cancer
institution: Stanford
data_notes: ID8 ovarian model + aPD-1, 14 Gy abdominopelvic FLASH. Transient weight/stool reduction, full recovery by day 17, no CBC difference vs FLASH+IgG. Immune: reduced Treg/Teff ratio, increased CD8+ TILs, reduced PMN-MDSC and M2/M1 macrophage ratio. Superior antitumor efficacy vs FLASH+IgG. No DMF calculable.

citation_title: Abdominopelvic FLASH Irradiation Improves PD-1 Immune Checkpoint Inhibition in Preclinical Models of Ovarian Cancer
institution: Stanford
data_notes: ID8 ovarian model, 14 Gy abdominopelvic FLASH, no co-tx. >2x regenerating jejunal crypts vs CONV at 96h. Tumor weight/ascites reduced equally by FLASH and CONV. Immune (96h): FLASH increased intratumoral CD4+ T cells vs CONV. Immune (17d): Both arms increased cytolytic CD107a+CD8+ T cells vs sham; no FLASH superiority at late timepoint. No DMF calculable.

citation_title: Dose, dose rate and split dose impacts murine skin responses following photon FLASH irradiation
notes_free_text: Dose, dose rate and split dose impacts murine skin responses following photon FLASH irradiation (2025) FLASH-SARRP Queen’s university Belfast and John Hopkins ST HL:7-9 week old BL57/6J, 4 m 4 f per group Tumour xenograft: 10-12 week injected flank CONV: 1 cm x 1 cm field, 20.2+_0.5 Gy FLASH: 1 cm x 2 cm field, 18.1+-0.9, 21.3+_1.2, 25.8+_1.4 Gy SPLIT FLASH: 1 cm x 2 cm field, 17.7+-0.5, 22.7+-0.6 Gy 3.2 Gy/min CONV skin/tumour (240,000 ms continuous), 72 Gy/s skin/81 Gy/s tumour (1 pulse, 250 ms/320 ms/400 ms) FLASH And FLASH split dose ( avg DR = 2.8 Gy/s skin, 1.9 Gy/s tumour, 2 pulses, 125 and 160 ms manually delivered 8-10 s apart) Skin toxicity delayed for FLASH and reduced hyperplasia and significant fibrosis deposition for FLASH vs conv. Tissue recovery for both at 8 weeks. Dose depedent relationship for FLASH sparing. Effect lost with split dose. Isoeffective tumour control. 3% isofluorane 500 ml/min for induction and 1 % 100ml/min for maintenance in room air YES

Relapse: FLASH 1/4=25%, CONV 3/3=100%, nonirradiated all relapsed. Effect_size: 75 pp reduction in relapse with FLASH vs CONV. Normal HSPC not detectable in secondary mice from either FLASH or CONV mixed grafts. FLASH preserved tumor control benefit but NOT hematopoietic sparing seen in Arm 6. Suggests leukemia presence negates the HSPC-sparing effect observed in pure CD34 model. Clinically relevant: shows complexity of using FLASH-TBI in active leukemia setting.
data_notes: Mixed CD34+/M106 co-engraftment, secondary transplant. Leukemia relapse: FLASH 1/4 vs CONV 3/3 (-75 pp). FLASH anti-leukemia benefit confirmed. No normal human hematopoiesis detected after FLASH-cell secondary transplant — no normal-tissue sparing scored here. Normal hematopoiesis likely outcompeted by residual leukemia.

citation_title: FLASH Radiotherapy Mitigates Radiation-Induced Lymphopenia and Prevents Immunosuppression via Chk1-STAT3 Axis Modulation in a Preclinical Thoracic Irradiation Model
institution: MD_Anderson_Cancer_Center
notes_free_text: Tao et al. 2025. MD Anderson. Mobetron 9 MeV electron. ARM 1: single-fraction FLASH 17 Gy. 7 pulses, DPP=2.43 Gy, DR=340 Gy/s, inst DR=1.4 MGy/s, pulse_width=1.9 µs, PRF=120 Hz, irr_time=0.05 s. Female C57BL/6, 8 wks, isoflurane, 4x2 cm2 field, whole thorax. Reduced fibrosis (4-5x less collagen vs CONV at 6mo); lymphocytes 1.2-6.5x higher, apoptosis 1.5-6.1x lower, PD-1/PD-L1 1.9-4.1x lower, Tregs 2.3-2.9x lower, pChk1/pSTAT3 signaling attenuated vs CONV. DMF not calculable. is_preprint=1 (session context; verify if published).
data_notes: 17 Gy thorax, single fraction. FLASH prevented prolonged lymphopenia and immunosuppression. CONV caused sustained Treg elevation and PD-1/PD-L1 upregulation. FLASH immune profiles near baseline. Chk1-STAT3 axis modulation identified as mechanism. No DMF.

data_notes: 10 Gy total (2 Gy x5 fx), fractionated thoracic. FLASH again prevented prolonged immunosuppression equivalent to single-fraction arm. Consistent immune protection across both fractionation regimens. Chk1-STAT3 axis modulation identified as mechanism. No DMF.

citation_title: Dose- and Volume-Limiting Late Toxicity of FLASH Radiotherapy in Cats with Squamous Cell Carcinoma of the Nasal Planum and in Mini Pigs
institution: University of Zurich / Lausanne University Hospital / University of Lausanne
data_notes: CAT ARM — NEGATIVE FLASH RESULT. 30 Gy x1 FLASH (1500 Gy/s, 20ms, 3 pulses) vs 10x4.8 Gy CONV (~6 Gy/min). 3/7 FLASH cats developed grade 3 late toxicity (bone/mucosal/skin necrosis, 9-15 months); 0/9 CONV cats. institution: Lund University;University of Copenhagen;Skåne University Hospital;DTU Health Tech;University of Oxford
notes_free_text: Konradsson 2021 (Front Oncol). Veterinary clinical FLASH feasibility. Modified Elekta Precise linac 10 MeV electrons. 10 dogs, 13 treatment arms. No conventional comparator. notes_free_text: Favaudon 2019 cat nasal planum SCC. Phase I single-dose escalation. 28.0 Gy FLASH electron (Oriatron 6e prototype). Dose rate not reported for cat arms. Field 34x45mm, direct contact. Tumor stage: T3_large_ulcerative. n=1 cat. No concurrent CONV arm (historical comparison only). Cat 3. Large ulcerative T3. No mucositis, no erythema. CR at 3 months, clinical recurrence 6-8 months, euthanized at 8 months. Suggests 28 Gy insufficient for some T3 disease.
data_notes: 28 Gy cat SCC nasal planum, single-arm FLASH. Initial complete response; recurrence 6-8 months. No acute toxicity. Late toxicity L0.

citation_title: Pencil beam scanning proton FLASH maintains tumor control while normal tissue damage is reduced in a mouse model
institution: Danish Centre for Particle Therapy, Aarhus University Hospital; Department of Experimental Clinical Oncology, Aarhus University Hospital; DTU Health Tech, Roskilde; Department of Oncology, Aarhus University Hospital, Denmark
notes_free_text: Sorensen 2022. CDF1 female, C3H mammary carcinoma hind limb foot. Single-fraction PBS proton dose-response (40–60 Gy range). No anesthesia during irradiation. ProBeam Varian. FLASH: 250 MeV, 71–89 Gy/s (mean 83), DRPBS95%=171–214 Gy/s, irr=0.46–0.84 s, n=70/75/35. CONV: 244 MeV, 0.33–0.63 Gy/s (mean 0.38), irr=95–173 s, n=72/80/36. FLASH 215 nA, no repainting; CONV 1.1 nA, 327–488 repaintings. Tumor control: TCD50_FLASH=51.3 Gy vs TCD50_CONV=49.1 Gy → DMF=1.04, p=0.50 (NS). Acute skin: MDD50_FLASH=52.3 Gy vs CONV MDD50 <40.1 Gy → DMF≥1.30 (acute). Fibrosis: FD50_FLASH=55.6 Gy vs FD50_CONV=48.6 Gy → DMF=1.14, p=0.0001. Fibrosis DMF (1.14) significantly less than published acute-skin DMF (1.50, p<0.05). Entrance plateau region (low LET); no SOBP effects. Beam geometry: entrance depth 13.5 cm, brass body shield, no collimator. Fibrosis analysis: only tumor-control mice included; limited n per dose group (limitation).
data_notes: PBS proton FLASH acute skin toxicity arm. MDD50 (score 3.5) shifted: CONV <40.1 Gy vs FLASH 52.3 Gy. DMF_acute >=1.30 (MDD50 ratio). Scale max=3.5 moist desquamation. Dose range: FLASH 40.4-60.2 Gy, CONV 40.1-59.7 Gy.

citation_title: FLASH with carbon ions: Tumor control, normal tissue sparing, and distal metastasis in a mouse osteosarcoma model
institution: GSI Helmholtzzentrum fur Schwerionenforschung, Darmstadt, Germany
followup_time: 28_days (tumor day 24; lung metastasis day 35 after injection)
notes_free_text: Tinganelli 2022 F1. LM8 osteosarcoma subcutaneous posterior limb, C3H/He female mice. 18 Gy single-fraction carbon-ion FLASH (~100 Gy/s, 1 spill, 150±20 ms) vs CONV (~0.3 Gy/s, many spills). GSI SIS18, 240 MeV/u 12C, pencil beam scanning, 20x20mm, bilateral limbs simultaneously. LET 14.5-15.5 keV/µm (entrance/plateau, not SOBP). n=7 FLASH, n=7 CONV, n=11 sham. Tumor: ~30% smaller avg size with FLASH vs CONV (author-reported). Muscle histology: reduced myofibril/nuclei disorganization with FLASH. Lung metastasis: FLASH significantly reduced vs CONV (p=0.011) and vs sham (p=0.00086). Distinct from Tinganelli 2025 (SOBP, LET 65-85 keV/um).
data_notes: 15 Gy carbon ion entrance channel, HIT. FLASH ~100 Gy/s vs CONV ~0.1 Gy/s. FLASH preserved muscle histological structure significantly better than CONV. Moderate LET (entrance plateau, not Bragg peak). No DMF calculable: fixed-dose histology study.

citation_title: FLASH Bragg-Peak Irradiation With a Therapeutic Carbon Ion Beam: First In Vivo Results
institution: GSI Helmholtzzentrum fur Schwerionenforschung, Darmstadt
notes_free_text: Tinganelli 2025. First in vivo FLASH with carbon ion SOBP (high-LET). GSI SIS18 synchrotron, 240 MeV/u 12C. LM8 osteosarcoma female C3H/He mice. 20 Gy UHDR (>100 Gy/s, ~40 ms) vs CDR. n=14 UHDR, n=15 CDR, n=10 sham. Significantly lower muscle fibrosis (collagen deposition) in UHDR vs CDR. Equivalent tumor control. SOBP LET 65-85 keV/um. Multi-endpoint: fibrosis, immune, microbiome, lung metastases. First evidence FLASH sparing persists at high LET carbon SOBP.
data_notes: 20 Gy single-fraction carbon ion SOBP, 240 MeV/u, GSI SIS18 synchrotron. LET 65-85 keV/um (therapeutic Bragg peak — NOT entrance plateau). FLASH >100 Gy/s (~40 ms local delivery) vs CONV standard synchrotron rate. 15x15 mm² field, 8x8 spot grid, 3 mm spacing. LM8 osteosarcoma hind-limb model. Skin score 0-2. FLASH: 21% score 0, remainder score 1. CONV: 93% score 1, 7% score 2. No significant skin toxicity difference between FLASH and CONV. HTE=NO.

citation_title: The sparing effect of FLASH-RT on synaptic plasticity is maintained in mice with standard fractionation
institution: Lausanne University Hospital; University of California Irvine
notes_free_text: Limoli 2023 (Radiother Oncol 186:109767). 10x3 Gy standard fractionation WBI, 6 MeV Oriatron eRT6. FLASH: 1.6e6 Gy/s, 1 pulse per fraction at 1.8 µs, DPP=3 Gy. CONV: 0.09 Gy/s, 33.3 s per fraction. Female C57BL/6J, 11 weeks, n=6/group. Follow-up 4 months. FLASH preserved LTP in both hippocampus and mPFC; CONV significantly inhibited LTP. Hippocampus: ANOVA F(2,15)=21.89 P<0.0001; FLASH vs CONV P<0.0001. mPFC: ANOVA F(2,12)=31 P<0.0001; FLASH vs CONV P<0.0001. Key finding: FLASH sparing of synaptic plasticity maintained under standard fractionation. Brain endpoints may retain FLASH sparing under multi-fraction schedules more than other tissues.
data_notes: 10x3 Gy WBI, 6 MeV electrons, Oriatron eRT6. FLASH: 1.6e6 Gy/s, 1 pulse/fraction at 1.8 µs, DPP=3 Gy. CONV: 0.09 Gy/s, 33.3 s/fraction. Female C57BL/6J 11 weeks, n=6/group. Follow-up 4 months. FLASH preserved LTP (hippocampus and mPFC); CONV impaired LTP. HTE=YES. Exception to global fractionation suppression: CNS/brain endpoint retains sparing. ENDPOINT: hippocampus fEPSP LTP. ANOVA F(2,15)=21.89 P<0.0001; Control vs CONV P=0.0002; FLASH vs CONV P<0.0001.
fractionation_notes: 10x3Gy standard fractionation Mon-Fri over 2 weeks; 1 pulse/fraction

CONV: 0.09 Gy/s, 33.3 s per fraction. Female C57BL/6J, 11 weeks, n=6/group. Follow-up 4 months. FLASH preserved LTP in both hippocampus and mPFC; CONV significantly inhibited LTP. Hippocampus: ANOVA F(2,15)=21.89 P<0.0001; FLASH vs CONV P<0.0001. mPFC: ANOVA F(2,12)=31 P<0.0001; FLASH vs CONV P<0.0001. Key finding: FLASH sparing of synaptic plasticity maintained under standard fractionation. Brain endpoints may retain FLASH sparing under multi-fraction schedules more than other tissues.
data_notes: 10x3 Gy WBI, 6 MeV electrons, Oriatron eRT6. FLASH: 1.6e6 Gy/s, 1 pulse/fraction at 1.8 µs, DPP=3 Gy. CONV: 0.09 Gy/s, 33.3 s/fraction. Female C57BL/6J 11 weeks, n=6/group. Follow-up 4 months. FLASH preserved LTP (hippocampus and mPFC); CONV impaired LTP. HTE=YES. Exception to global fractionation suppression: CNS/brain endpoint retains sparing. ENDPOINT: medial prefrontal cortex fEPSP LTP. ANOVA F(2,12)=31 P<0.0001; Control vs CONV P=0.0002; FLASH vs CONV P<0.0001.
fractionation_notes: 10x3Gy standard fractionation Mon-Fri over 2 weeks; 1 pulse/fraction
